changes_made: Renamed k8s_audit_policy_captures_metadata to k8s_audit_policy_metadata_capture_enabled for consistency
review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security monitoring capabilities. Audit logging and admission controllers are appropriate for monitoring system interactions. However, the function name for capturing metadata in audit policies was adjusted for clarity and consistency with naming conventions. The service mesh function was removed as it is not a standard Kubernetes resource and its security monitoring capabilities are not universally applicable.
technical_concerns: k8s_service_mesh_security_monitoring_enabled is not a standard Kubernetes function and may not be applicable across all environments.
naming_improvements: Ensure function names clearly indicate the action and follow the pattern k8s_<resource>_<check_description>.
title: System Monitoring - Monitor the system to detect:...
step1_reasoning: The control SI-4-a focuses on system monitoring to detect security threats. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to enabling audit logging to monitor API server interactions, ensuring audit policies capture necessary metadata for detection, and leveraging admission controllers and service mesh for security monitoring. These components help in detecting and responding to security events within a Kubernetes cluster.
